title: QUIC RFC 9002 Appendix A Helper Recovery-State Work Item
summary: Close the helper-backed Appendix A recovery-state subset with focused requirement-home evidence, deterministic fuzz top-offs, and existing benchmark support without broadening runtime-owned recovery behavior.
planned_changes:
- Split the generated Appendix A review's `move now` set into a bounded helper-only RFC 9002 artifact trio.
- Keep the existing broad recovery runtime artifact untouched and avoid claiming deferred Appendix A sender/runtime clauses.
- Add deterministic fuzz/property top-offs for the shared RTT adjustment, loss-detection timer priority, and loss-delay helper arithmetic.
- Run the targeted requirement-home tests for the 23 selected helper-backed clauses.
- Run existing BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation for the relevant RTT and congestion helper suites as supporting performance evidence only.
verification_plan: Run the focused Appendix A helper-backed requirement-home filter for SAP2, SAP7, SAP8 minus SAP8-0005, and SAP10. Run BenchmarkDotNet Dry for existing `QuicRttEstimatorBenchmarks` and `QuicCongestionControlBenchmarks` without editing benchmark source. Finish with SpecTrace render/check, `git diff --check`, and the known-baseline core SpecTrace validation profile.
completion_notes: The helper subset is closed by existing positive/negative/edge requirement-home coverage plus deterministic fuzz top-offs for the RTT, timer, and loss-delay helper seams. No production code or benchmark source was changed.
out_of_scope: SAP1, SAP1P1, SAP4, SAP5, SAP6, SAP8-0005, SAP9, and SAP11 Appendix A clauses.
Public API or interop harness behavior.
New benchmark implementation or public performance comparison claims.
